title: uragoga grayana
side_effects: Uragoga grayana can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach pain, which can be caused by excessive consumption of its extracts. Prolonged use may also lead to liver damage, headaches, and fatigue, as the plant contains toxic compounds that can harm the body. In severe cases, uragoga grayana can cause respiratory problems, allergic reactions, and even kidney damage, highlighting the importance of proper dosage and supervision. It is crucial to consult a medical professional before using uragoga grayana to minimize the risk of these side effects.
